Space Force adds cyber units to guard rocket launches

‘Cyber defense’ squadrons at Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg reflect growing concern over malicious hacks The post Space Force adds cyber units to guard rocket launches appeared first on SpaceNews.

Space Force cyber defense units now protect launch infrastructure from digital threats, recognizing that operational security extends beyond physical perimeters to networked systems controlling critical aerospace missions.

The Real Reason Why Boeing Is Building The F-47 Stealth Fighter And Not Lockheed

The jet will be built on stealth, speed, and strategy.

Boeing's F-47 development signals a strategic pivot to compete directly in advanced fighter procurement, leveraging its defense contracts and manufacturing capacity against Lockheed's traditional dominance in stealth platforms.

Confirmed: This Is Emirates' Only US Route That's Still Suspended

Eight in ten travelers transit via Dubai from Orlando to other international destinations

Emirates' Orlando suspension reflects strategic hub-and-spoke vulnerability when single US gateways underperform, forcing passengers to reroute through competing hubs.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents develop.

Regulatory Mar 23, 2026

FAA approves higher takeoff weights for Boeing 787-9 and 787-10

Boeing said on March 23, 2026, that the Federal Aviation Administration has certified increased maximum takeoff weight options… The post FAA approves higher takeoff weights for Boeing 787-9 and 787-10 appeared first on AeroTime.

Higher certified takeoff weights enable Boeing widebodies to carry additional payload or fuel, directly improving route economics and operational flexibility for carriers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 23, 2026

Extra Security: Why All US F-14 Tomcats Were Destroyed In The Arizona Desert

How the US ensured that F-14 parts woudn't end up in enemy hands.

The systematic destruction of retired F-14s in Arizona's desert boneyard prevented adversaries from acquiring advanced variable-sweep wing technology and tactical systems through salvage or theft.

See Simple Flying →

Two of the three encounters took place over the weekend.

Rising incidents of ground-to-air fire against law enforcement helicopters underscore critical vulnerabilities in low-altitude rotorcraft operations and highlight urgent need for enhanced defensive protocols.

Military Mar 23, 2026

110 Soldiers Onboard: Lockheed C-130 Crashes

The Lockheed C-130 Hercules, a staple of military aviation, crashed in Colombia's Amazon region, leaving many questions about the cause.

The C-130's crash in remote terrain underscores operational risks when operating aging military transport aircraft in challenging environments with limited emergency infrastructure.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts

Amazon vowed to double the annual launch rate for its low Earth orbit broadband constellation to more than 20 missions, hinging largely on rockets yet to prove themselves at scale. The post Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts appeared first on SpaceNews.

Amazon's Leo constellation success depends on unproven rocket platforms delivering at unprecedented scale, creating significant schedule risk if launch providers face delays.

ICE deployment to security checkpoints signals TSA's inability to maintain screening operations independently, creating operational and customer experience risks across the commercial aviation network.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9 depa

The Mustang 9 departure from RNO's parallel runways 17L/R requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural constraints that could impact climb performance and routing compliance.

Why This Airline Isn't Worried About The Boeing 777X's Lengthy Delays

While many airlines have frantically altered plans due to constant Boeing delays, one airline is remaining totally calm, proceeding in strength.

Airlines with diversified aircraft portfolios and strong balance sheets can absorb 777X delays without operational disruption, while those dependent on single aircraft types face escalating competitive disadvantages.

Military transport operations face critical safety risks during initial climb phases when aircraft are at maximum weight and minimum altitude for recovery.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Science Through Shadows: How Astronomical Alignments Reveal the Universe

When one celestial object passes in front of another, it can cast a shadow that travels across space – and sometimes across Earth. These moments of alignment, known as eclipses, occultations, and transits, allow scientists to study distant objects in remarkable ways. By observing how light change...

Celestial alignment observation techniques used in astronomy directly inform aircraft navigation and satellite positioning systems critical to modern aviation operations.

See NASA →

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use t

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from trainers to air carriers positions it as a critical regional hub for both pilot development and commercial connectivity in the Midwest.

Drones Mar 23, 2026

Radiate Engineering & Design introduces FlyBlocks – a production-ready structuralairframe platform for long-range VTOL UAV applications

Radiate Engineering & Design AG, based in Zurich, introduces FlyBlocks, a production-ready structuralairframe platform for

FlyBlocks addresses the critical manufacturing gap in commercial VTOL drone development by offering a pre-engineered, modular airframe that accelerates time-to-market for operators.

Industry Mar 23, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through technology integration offers immediate efficiency gains while research gaps remain in scaling sustainable aviation solutions across the industry's complex infrastructure.

See MDPI →

Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market

Hybrid antenna developed by Parsons and Raven Defense combines a six-meter parabolic dish with an electronically steered phased-array feed The post Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market appeared first on SpaceNews.

Parsons' hybrid antenna combining mechanically steered dishes with electronic phased-array technology addresses military demand for flexible satellite communications with reduced latency and increased coverage.

Military Mar 23, 2026

Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster

Tragedy struck on March 23, 1994, at Pope Air Force Base, North Carolina, on an aircraft parking ramp known as ‘Green Ramp.’ A number of paratroopers were lost and several injured after flames and debris rained down on their training and assembly area after a mid-air collision between a fighter j...

The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers when an F-16 collided with a transport plane, driving systemic safety reforms in military airfield operations and low-altitude training protocols.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami

A new data visualization illustrates how an experimental NASA technology can provide extra lead time to communities in the path of a tsunami. Called GUARDIAN (GNSS Upper Atmospheric Real-time Disaster Information and Alert Network), the software detects slight distortions in satellite navigation ...

GUARDIAN's real-time satellite navigation detection could enable aircraft and maritime operations to receive earlier tsunami warnings, improving emergency response coordination for aviation assets in coastal regions.

See NASA →

Sovereign demand and institutional capital reshape space economy

The post Sovereign demand and institutional c...

Institutional capital inflows into space ventures signal market maturation, enabling early-stage aerospace startups to pursue scalable mega-projects previously limited to government-backed programs.

Real-time satellite hydrology data from SWOT enables aerospace-derived Earth observation applications that can revolutionize water resource management and climate monitoring for global sustainability.

We are now in the fourth week since the launch of Operation Epic Fury and the Israeli counterpart...

Sustained U.S. military aviation deployment to the Middle East signals extended operational commitment beyond initial response phases, requiring strategic airlift coordination and extended logistics planning.

NASA’s Hubble, Webb Telescopes Survey Pinwheel Galaxy

This March 16, 2026, image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope and the James Webb Space Telescope takes a closer look at the core of Messier 101, also known as the Pinwheel Galaxy. At 25 million light-years away, M101 is one of the closest “face-on” spiral galaxies to us. With that in mind, Hubble...

Advanced space telescopes enable unprecedented multi-wavelength observation capabilities that enhance structural understanding of distant galactic phenomena.
